claims.evidence_text: While test weights of 56 pounds per bushel are most desirable, lighter test weights may be an acceptable alternative when heavier seed of desirable varieties is not available.
claims.claim_summary: Most desirable wheat seed test weight is 56 lb/bu

claims.evidence_text: Yields and vigor are likely to be affected at test weights below 54-56 pounds per bushel. Below that level, the plants are likely to have more problems in the fall and in surviving the winter.
claims.claim_summary: Yield and vigor affected at seed test weight below 54-56 lb/bu

claims.evidence_text: Plant low-test-weight seed 1 to 1.5 inches deep.
claims.claim_summary: Plant low-test-weight wheat seed 1 to 1.5 inches deep

claims.evidence_text: Days to emergence 21 27 ... Higher test weight seed emerged 4-6 days sooner.
claims.claim_summary: Days to emergence 21-27; heavier seed emerged 4-6 days sooner

claims.evidence_text: Emergence (% of viable seeds) 60.8% 28.4% ... Emergence (% of viable seeds) 68.0% 48.4% ... Higher test weight seed had 20-40% improved field emergence.
claims.claim_summary: Field emergence ranged 28-68% of viable seeds; heavier seed 20-40% higher
